mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below its long-run neutral estimate (near 3%) signals continued monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations via lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 2%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% in Jan 2017, well below historical neutral rate
regime_hint: any regime with low policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of 0.3 percentage points or more from its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals an emerging labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7-4.8% in early 2017, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal shows no significant difference from unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing confirmation often reflects transitory spending shifts.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST near 1226-1252k units and RSAFS near 464-472B in late 2016/early 2017
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression for industrial firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in early-cycle regimes the same deceleration is noise because it reflects normalization rather than exhaustion of capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 104.5548 vs revised 98.7611 indicating measurement uncertainty in early 2017 readings
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated capacity utilization
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle windows, negative INDPRO growth episodes are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion and household income growth that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,554-145,628 thousand persons in Jan 2017, continuing multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and forward 5d returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no meaningful short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5% following a prolonged near-zero period, it signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that initially raises uncertainty about future discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until markets fully price the new policy path.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5% after being below 0.5% for at least 12 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose to 0.65% in Dec 2016 following near-zero rates since 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d forward returns in more than 55% of instances immediately following the crossing trigger, or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct tightening cycle in the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real policy rate, proxied by the joint condition of increasing FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation, signals a genuine tightening of financial conditions that erodes real consumer purchasing power and corporate discount-adjusted valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; FEDFUNDS increases alone are insufficient if inflation is rising in tandem since real rates remain flat.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth decelerates by more than 0.3pp
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 244.158 vs revised 243.618 in Jan 2017 amid rising FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 60d SPX returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock industrial recovery regime, a sharp rebound in INDPRO growth signals rapid restocking and capacity utilization increases that boost near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; outside recovery regimes the same rebound magnitude carries no informational content because it reflects routine volatility.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO index near 98.76-104.55 in early 2017 following prior industrial soft patch in 2015-2016
failure_condition: During identified recovery-regime windows, INDPRO rebound episodes are followed by negative 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
